differential_diagnoses: Insulin receptor antibody syndrome (Type B insulin resistance)
Severe insulin-resistant diabetes mellitus secondary to underlying autoimmunity
next_steps: Measure serum insulin-receptor autoantibodies (specifically for Type B insulin resistance)
Quantify serum free and total insulin levels (to evaluate for anti-insulin antibodies)
Genetic testing for lipodystrophy-associated mutations (e.g., AGPAT2, BSCL2, CAV1)
Assess for complement levels (C3, C4) and associated autoimmune diseases (e.g., SLE)
Evaluate for underlying lymphoproliferative disorders or malignancy (further investigation of the splenic lesion)
Initiate referral to a specialized center for metabolic disorders and lipodystrophy management
Continuous glucose monitoring to assess for glycemic variability and verify insulin administration patterns

differential_diagnoses: Combined lipodystrophic syndrome and autoantibody-mediated insulin resistance
next_steps: Measure insulin-receptor autoantibodies (testing for Type B insulin resistance)
Perform a specialized clinical and laboratory evaluation for systemic autoimmunity (SLE panel including dsDNA, Smith, RNP, Ro/La)
Consultation with Hematology/Oncology for comprehensive reassessment of the splenic lesion and bone marrow fibrosis (evaluating for occult malignancy or clonal process)
Consultation with a Metabolic Bone/Lipodystrophy specialist for management of severe insulin resistance and potential use of leptin replacement therapy
Continuous glucose monitoring (CGM) to characterize glycemic variability
Review and re-interpretation of the bone marrow biopsy by hematopathology for evidence of B-cell lymphoproliferative disease
